questions.question: Explain 1's complement and 2's complement representations for signed binary numbers. Also explain how zero is represented in both methods and mention one advantage of 2's complement over 1's complement.
keyPoints: 1's complement is obtained by inverting all bits of the binary number
2's complement is obtained by adding 1 to 1's complement of the number
In 1's complement, zero has two representations (+0 and -0), while in 2's complement, zero has unique representation
2's complement simplifies arithmetic operations as no special handling for carry is required

questions.question: Subtract (10011)_2 from (11001)_2 using 2's complement method. Show all steps clearly and verify the result.
keyPoints: Add the 2's complement to the minuend
If there is a final carry, discard it to get the result
If there is no final carry, take 2's complement of the result and prefix with negative sign

questions.question: (a) Explain the step-by-step procedure for subtracting two binary numbers using 1's complement method. (b) Perform subtraction: (10101)_2 - (01110)_2 using 1's complement method. (c) Explain the conditions for detecting overflow in complement arithmetic and illustrate with an example where overflow occurs.
keyPoints: 1's complement subtraction: add 1's complement of subtrahend to minuend, if there's a carry, add it to the result (end-around carry)
Overflow in complement arithmetic occurs when the signs of the two numbers are same but the sign of the result is different
For n-bit numbers, overflow occurs when carry into sign bit and carry out of sign bit are different
In the example, when adding two positive numbers gives negative result or two negative numbers gives positive result, overflow has occurred

questions.question: (a) Convert the decimal number 192 to binary, octal, and hexadecimal representations. (b) Explain sign-magnitude representation for signed numbers and list two major limitations of this method. (c) Perform the subtraction (10010)_2 - (11001)_2 using 2's complement method and verify if overflow has occurred, explaining your reasoning.
keyPoints: 192 in binary = 11000000, in octal = 300, in hexadecimal = C0
Sign-magnitude uses MSB as sign bit (0 for positive, 1 for negative) and remaining bits for magnitude; limitations include dual representation of zero and complex arithmetic operations
Overflow detection: when subtracting using 2's complement, overflow occurs when the signs of the operands are different and the sign of result differs from the sign of the minuend
In this case, minuend is positive and subtrahend is negative in terms of actual operation, requiring careful bit-level analysis
